title: Whole-Body Bone Scintigraphy Research
subtitle: From annotation to thesis: a 3-year research arc
reflection: I learned to fully retrain a segmentation model (nnU-Net v2), not just fine-tune it. Setting up the full training pipeline from data format to inference is a different task.
I learned to process medical imaging data, including DICOM files, and understand what clinicians actually look at when they read a scan.
I learned to coordinate with teammates across different backgrounds: coders, doctors, thesis supervisors. Keeping the work moving is its own skill.
Annotating data manually teaches you exactly where models fail.
I contributed to building Telplastina: an application that doctors can open and use. That is different from writing a paper about it.

highlights: Got assigned as lead coder on a Ministry of Higher Education-funded project. No prior WBBS experience.
Annotated 600+ bone scan images in IbisPaintX, every skeletal region manually, supervised by nuclear medicine physicians at Dr. Hasan Sadikin General Hospital, Universitas Padjadjaran.
Wrote the detection and segmentation code: YOLOv8 for hotspot localization, nnU-Net v2 across 12 skeletal regions.
Built the BSI quantification pipeline. Results surpassed both commercial systems in clinical use.
Started visiting the hospital directly. Worked with nuclear medicine doctors to understand how BSI is actually scored in the clinic.
Grew the dataset significantly: anterior and posterior views, 12 regions each.
Participated in building Telplastina v1, the first time this pipeline became an actual application.
Submitted the first preprint to SSRN in January 2025.
Turned 2 years of research into my bachelor's thesis.
Led development of Telplastina v2: Vue 3 frontend, Rust backend, Docker deployment.
Journal paper accepted in IJIES-INASS. Dataset released on Zenodo, open to anyone.
